free_text: Proton induced reactions on 114Sn and 120Sn targets
at energies up to 18 MeV
Institute of Applied Problems of Physics,
Yerevan, Armenia
Cyclotron C18/18
Stack of enriched 114Sn and 120Sn foils were used.
The stack was composed of 6 blocks of natCu-114Sn-
natCu-120Sn layers where tin foils were 20 to 40 mu-m
thick and copper foils were 20 mu-m thick.
High-purity germanium detector GEM15P4-70 was
used. The energy resolution of the HPGe detector was
1.66 keV FWHM at 1332.5 keV peak of 60Co, and 0.618 keV
FWHM at 122 keV peak of 57Co.
Irradiation was 5 min long with a collimated
1 mu-A proton beam of the same diameter as the
target. The measurements were performed periodically
during several days, with the first measurement done
40 min after the end of irradiation (to allow
short-lived isotope cross-section measurements).
The proton energy uncertainties were calculated using
SRIM-2013 code and also include uncertainties from the
primary beam energy (0.2 MeV) and target thickness (5%)
Resulting cross section uncertainties add
uncertainties from the following sources in quadrature:
Statistical errors on counts
Uncertainty in the flux
Uncertainty in detector efficiency
Uncertainty in target thickness
Entry was approved by Dr. G.H.Hovhannisyan
*By authors* Reaction cross sections were measured on
a 114Sn target with an enrichment of 63.2%. The data
presented are corrected for 100% enrichment.
The contribution of 111In(p,2p)111(m+g)In can be
neglected.
Tbl. 3

*By authors* Reaction cross sections were measured on
a 114Sn target with an enrichment of 63.2%. The data
presented are corrected for 100% enrichment.
Contributions of other Sn isotopes contained in the
target to the 113Sn production can be neglected.
Tbl. 3
*By authors* Reaction cross sections were measured on
a 114Sn target with an enrichment of 63.2%. The data
presented are corrected for 100% enrichment.
The contribution of the side reaction
112Sn(p,gamma)113Sb does not exceed the uncertainty
of measured cross section.
Tbl. 3
*By authors* The purity of the 120Sn target used in the
experiments is 99.6%, making the contributions of
other tin isotopes to product yields negligibly small.
